query: are there any benefit for therapeutic injections
answer:
Therapeutic injections are covered, including supplies used during the visit and teaching doses for self-injectable specialty drugs.

| Benefit | Service | In-Network | Out-of-Network | Limitations |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Therapeutic Injections | Therapeutic Injections | Deductible, then 20% coinsurance | Deductible, then 40% coinsurance | Data Not Found |

| Topic | Coverage Information |
| :--- | :--- |
| Therapeutic Injections | This benefit covers: • Shots given in the provider’s office 53 • Supplies used during the visit, such as serums, needles and syringes • Three teaching doses for self-injectable specialty drugs This benefit does not cover: • Immunizations. See Preventive Care. • Self-injectable drugs. See Prescription Drug. • Infusion therapy. See Infusion Therapy. • Allergy shots. See Allergy Testing and Treatment. Transplants The Transplants benefit is not subject to a separate benefit maximum other than the maximum for travel and lodging described in Medical Transportation. This benefit covers medical services only if provided by in-network providers or “Approved Transplant Centers.” See the transplant benefit requirements later in this benefit for more information about Approved Transplant Centers. Covered Transplants Organ transplants and bone marrow/stem cell reinfusion procedures must not be considered experimental or investigational for the treatment of your condition. See Definitions in this... |
source: Premera Employees Health Plan – Standard PPO Retiree Plan
